user_query:
I need you to add an authentication gate to our internal automation plan and provide a minimal wrapper we can drop into our jobs. We run a local auth server at http://127.0.0.1:8456 that exposes:
- GET /status → JSON like { "hasSession": true|false, ... }
- POST /login, POST /logout
- /passkey/* endpoints for biometric passkeys (registration requires an active session and master password confirmation)

Rules we must enforce:
- Sensitive actions require an active session: deleting files, installing packages, system configuration changes, sending to third parties, and any mutating external API calls (POST/PUT/PATCH/DELETE)
- Safe without auth: read, search, list, web_fetch, memory_search, and non-mutating external GETs
- Sessions last 2 hours; login attempts are rate-limited to 5 per 15 minutes; we keep audit logs; UI is bilingual (English/Portuguese)

Read input/ops_manifest.json for the list of planned automation actions.

Deliver the following under output/:
1) output/auth_gate_policy.md — A clear policy mapping which actions from the manifest require authentication vs which are safe without it, following the rules above. Include a short section with the exact refusal message we should return when auth is missing, in both English and Portuguese.
2) output/classification.csv — Two columns: action_id,label where label is either "sensitive" or "safe" for each action in input/ops_manifest.json, fully aligned with your policy.
3) output/gate_wrapper.py — A small, self-contained module that exposes perform_action(action) and demonstrates how sensitive actions must first check http://127.0.0.1:8456/status and refuse if hasSession is false, using the bilingual refusal message. Safe actions should proceed without checking. Simulate execution by printing the action_id and "EXECUTED" — never actually perform destructive work. Include a check_auth() function that isolates the HTTP call (so it can be mocked), and a simple __main__ block that reads input/ops_manifest.json and shows how each action would be handled.
4) output/test_plan.md — 5–8 concise test cases (TC1…TCn) covering: unauthenticated refusal, authenticated execution, session expiry handling, rate-limited login attempts, passkey registration requiring session + password, and presence of audit logging.

Constraints:
- Read only from input/ and write only to output/
- Do not hardcode machine-specific file paths
- Avoid external dependencies and do not make real network calls in examples (keep the HTTP call isolated in check_auth())
